conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child - my son Alex. He's 9 years old and lives with me here at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's good to know about Alex.

Now, to help determine your eligibility, I need to ask: Are you responsible for Alex? This means you're the person who is primarily caring for him and meeting his needs (such as providing food, clothing, and a place to live).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for Alex. I care for him, provide his food, clothing, and he lives with me. I'm the one looking after all his needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you for confirming that.

Next question: Is Alex currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. We both live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you.

Now I need to ask about your own circumstances: Are you currently living in the UK yourself?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I live here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One more important question: Do you have the right to reside in the UK? This would include being a UK citizen, having settled status, or having another immigration status that gives you the right to live here.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do have the right to reside in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for providing all that information.

Just to make sure I have a complete picture: Is there anyone else who is also responsible for Alex, such as another parent or guardian who shares care responsibilities with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, I'm the one Alex lives with and I'm his primary carer. He lives here with me full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. 

One final question to ensure I have all the necessary information: Is Alex in full-time non-advanced education or approved training? Given that he's 9 years old, I imagine he's likely in primary school, but I just want to confirm his current educational status.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - he's in primary school. He goes to school full-time like other children his age.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

You meet all the necessary criteria:
- Alex is under 16 years old (he's 9)
- You are responsible for him and he lives with you
- You provide for his care including food, clothing, and accommodation
- Both you and Alex live in the UK
- You have the right to reside in the UK
- Alex is in full-time education

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. If you haven't already done so, you can apply online or by post. You should claim as soon as possible, as Child Benefit can only be backdated for up to 3 months.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit: 1) He is under 16 years old (he is 9 years old), 2) He lives with the claimant who is responsible for him, 3) The claimant is responsible for Alex and provides for his care including food, clothing, and accommodation, 4) Both Alex and the claimant live in the UK, 5) The claimant has the right to reside in the UK, 6) Alex is in full-time education (primary school) which is appropriate for his age. All conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are satisfied for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their son Alex. The claimant is responsible for Alex who is 9 years old, lives with them in the UK, and attends primary school full-time. The claimant has the right to reside in the UK and meets all the necessary criteria for claiming Child Benefit.

All conditions for Child Benefit eligibility are satisfied for Alex.
